parameters.source: MY34/2018: onset Ls 184.9, intense phase ~3 months, decay into Ls ~250+ (Guzewich et al. 2019; Viudez-Moreiras et al. 2019); MY25/2001 similar
parameters.notes: Profile: ~1-2 week ramp-up, 30-60 sols near peak, 1-3 month exponential decay tail. Sizes energy storage / non-solar backup for settlements.

parameters.name: Dust deposition rate on horizontal surfaces (solar array obscuration)
parameters.source: Pathfinder 0.28%/sol (Landis & Jenkins); MER Spirit/Opportunity medians 0.16-0.18%/sol; InSight median 0.28%/sol; synthesis Lorenz et al. (2021) P&SS 207
parameters.notes: Upper end during/after storms. Episodic wind/dust-devil cleaning events recover tens of % (few per Mars year at MER sites) but are not schedulable - take no credit at L0/L1. InSight (no cleaning): ~90% array loss over ~4 Mars years. verified correction: the note's 'InSight ~90% array loss over ~4 Mars years' conflates Earth with Mars years - InSight operated ~4 Earth years = ~2.1 Mars years; using 4 Mars years understates the no-cleaning rate ~2x. InSight median deposition was 0.28%/sol (upper half of the envelope), not the 0.16-0.18 MER medians.

parameters.name: Dust devil areal formation rate (site/season dependent)
parameters.source: Jezero MEDA/RDS: 1.3-3.4 /km^2/sol (Toledo et al. 2023 JGR Planets); Pathfinder ~19; Spirit seasonal peak ~50; InSight: 502 vortex pressure drops >0.3 Pa in 151 sols
parameters.notes: Summer-midday peak. Point encounter with core pressure drop >=8 Pa: ~once per several hundred sols. Mechanically negligible (q<1 Pa); matters for array cleaning, electrostatics, sensor contamination.

parameters.name: Near-surface mean wind speed (1.5-2 m height)
parameters.source: Viking 0-9.5 m/s; InSight 0-7.5 m/s; Perseverance 0-12 m/s (Martinez et al. 2017 review; Viking/InSight/MEDA records)
parameters.notes: Diurnally modulated: calm nights, convective afternoons. Model as Weibull with diurnal modulation at L2.

parameters.name: Design gust wind speed (storm conditions)
parameters.source: Bickel et al. (2025), Sci. Adv. 11, eadw5170 (orbital dust-devil migration, supports 20-40 m/s near-surface winds); for in-situ MEDA gusts cite Hueso et al. (2023) JGR Planets / MEDA storm papers
parameters.notes: Dynamic pressure q = 0.5*rho*v^2 = ~9 Pa at 30 m/s with rho=0.020 kg/m^3 - a light Earth breeze. Wind force is NOT a structural driver; dust ingress/abrasion is. Convective h at habitat scale: 0.4 (still) to 4.0 (windy) W/m^2K (JPL MSR guideline via TFAWS 2024). verified correction: DOI 10.1126/sciadv.adw5170 is Bickel et al. 2025 (orbital dust-devil-migration wind inference), not a Perseverance MEDA in-situ 22 m/s gust measurement. The 30 m/s design value (range 22-40) is unchanged and supported by Bickel's inferred tens-of-m/s near-surface winds.

parameters.name: Total UV irradiance (200-380 nm) at surface, local noon
parameters.source: MSL REMS UV 10+ Mars-year record: total 26.9-42.3 W/m^2 (UVA 21.4-33.7, UVB 4.21-6.45, UVC 1.39-2.21), PNAS 2025
parameters.notes: UVC reaches the surface (no ozone layer): polymer degradation and surface sterilization. Attenuate as exp(-tau/cos z); dust roughly grey in UV-vis.

parameters.name: Regolith thermal conductivity (upper 0.4 m, unconsolidated)
parameters.source: InSight HP3 active heating experiment, 0.03-0.37 m depth: 0.039 +/- 0.002 W/m/K (Grott et al. 2021 JGR Planets)
parameters.notes: Flight-measured, one site; duricrust/rockier ground higher. Regolith is effectively insulation - ground-coupled heat rejection needs large area. Bulk density 1470-1730 kg/m^3 (HP3); cp ~630 J/kgK; albedo 0.105-0.315 (MCD v6.1), typical 0.20.

parameters.name: Regolith thermal inertia (typical soil, lander sites)
parameters.source: InSight HP3: 160-230 (Grott et al. 2021); MCD v6.1 engineering range 42-394 used in NASA TFAWS 2024 worst-case study
parameters.notes: Global orbital range ~24 (dust) to ~800 (rock/duricrust). Low TI = larger diurnal ground temperature swings. Diurnal thermal skin depth ~3.3 cm; seasonal ~0.9 m.

parameters.name: Regolith temperature at 1 m depth (annual mean, low-latitude site)
parameters.source: InSight HP3 (Elysium, 4.5 N): 217.5 K mean at 0.1-0.2 m, seasonal +/-6.7 K, diurnal damped (Spohn et al. 2024 GRL); extrapolated to 1 m where diurnal wave vanishes
parameters.notes: At 1 m: zero diurnal variation, seasonal amplitude ~30% of surface seasonal wave. Stable cold boundary for buried habitats/tanks; colder at higher latitude (roughly tracks annual-mean surface temperature).
